utf8.c(3) Library Functions Manual utf8.c(3)

NAME

utf8.c - 🔤 UTF-8 encoding and decoding with multi-byte character support

SYNOPSIS

Functions


int utf8_decode (const uint8_t *s, uint32_t *codepoint)
int utf8_display_width (const char *str)
int utf8_display_width_n (const char *str, size_t max_bytes)
size_t utf8_char_count (const char *str)
bool utf8_is_valid (const char *str)
bool utf8_is_ascii_only (const char *str)
size_t utf8_to_codepoints (const char *str, uint32_t *out_codepoints, size_t max_codepoints)
int utf8_next_char_bytes (const char *str, size_t max_bytes)
int utf8_continuation_bytes_needed (unsigned char first_byte)
int utf8_read_and_insert_continuation_bytes (char *buffer, size_t *cursor, size_t *len, size_t max_len, int continuation_bytes, int(*read_byte_fn)(void))
const char * utf8_strcasestr (const char *haystack, const char *needle)
Case-insensitive substring search with full Unicode support. void utf8_write_lowercase (FILE *output, const char *text)
Write UTF-8 string to file with ASCII lowercase conversion.

Detailed Description

🔤 UTF-8 encoding and decoding with multi-byte character support

Uses utf8proc Unicode library for accurate character-width computation and UTF-8 handling.

Definition in file utf8.c.

Function Documentation

size_t utf8_char_count (const char * str)

Definition at line 136 of file utf8.c.

136                                         {
137   if (!str) {
138     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "str is NULL");
139     return -1; // SIZE_MAX
140   }
141 
142   size_t count = 0;
143   const uint8_t *p = (const uint8_t *)str;
144   while (*p) {
145     uint32_t codepoint;
146     int decode_len = utf8_decode(p, &codepoint);
147     if (decode_len < 0) {
148       return -1; // SIZE_MAX - Invalid UTF-8
149     }
150     count++;
151     p += decode_len;
152   }
153   return count;
154 }




References SET_ERRNO, and utf8_decode().

  utf8_is_valid().






int
  utf8_continuation_bytes_needed (unsigned char first_byte)


Definition at line 222 of file utf8.c.


222                                                              {
223   if ((first_byte & 0x80) == 0) {
224     return 0; // ASCII (0xxxxxxx) - single byte, no continuation needed
225   }
226   if ((first_byte & 0xE0) == 0xC0) {
227     return 1; // 110xxxxx - 2 byte sequence, 1 continuation byte needed
228   }
229   if ((first_byte & 0xF0) == 0xE0) {
230     return 2; // 1110xxxx - 3 byte sequence, 2 continuation bytes needed
231   }
232   if ((first_byte & 0xF8) == 0xF0) {
233     return 3; // 11110xxx - 4 byte sequence, 3 continuation bytes needed
234   }
235   return -1; // Invalid UTF-8 start byte
236 }










int
  utf8_decode (const uint8_t * s, uint32_t * codepoint)


Definition at line 18 of file utf8.c.


18                                                        {
19   if (s[0] < 0x80) {
20     *codepoint = s[0];
21     return 1;
22   }
23   if ((s[0] & 0xE0) == 0xC0) {
24     // Validate continuation byte
25     if ((s[1] & 0xC0) != 0x80)
26       return -1;
27     *codepoint = (((uint32_t)(s[0] & 0x1F) << 6) | (uint32_t)(s[1] & 0x3F));
28     return 2;
29   } else if ((s[0] & 0xF0) == 0xE0) {
30     // Validate continuation bytes
31     if ((s[1] & 0xC0) != 0x80 || (s[2] & 0xC0) != 0x80)
32       return -1;
33     *codepoint = (((uint32_t)(s[0] & 0x0F) << 12) | ((uint32_t)(s[1] & 0x3F) << 6) | (uint32_t)(s[2] & 0x3F));
34     return 3;
35   } else if ((s[0] & 0xF8) == 0xF0) {
36     // Validate continuation bytes
37     if ((s[1] & 0xC0) != 0x80 || (s[2] & 0xC0) != 0x80 || (s[3] & 0xC0) != 0x80)
38       return -1;
39     *codepoint = (((uint32_t)(s[0] & 0x07) << 18) | ((uint32_t)(s[1] & 0x3F) << 12) | ((uint32_t)(s[2] & 0x3F) << 6) |
40                   (uint32_t)(s[3] & 0x3F));
41     return 4;
42   }
43   return -1; // Invalid
44 }

int
  utf8_display_width (const char * str)


Definition at line 46 of file utf8.c.


46                                         {
47   if (!str || !*str) {
48     return 0;
49   }
50 
51   int width = 0;
52   const utf8proc_uint8_t *p = (const utf8proc_uint8_t *)str;
53 
54   while (*p) {
55     // Skip ANSI escape sequences (ESC [ ... m)
56     // Pattern: \x1b [ [0-9;]* m
57     if (p[0] == 0x1b && p[1] == '[') {
58       // Found ANSI escape sequence start
59       p += 2; // Skip ESC[
60       // Skip digits and semicolons until we find 'm'
61       while (*p && *p != 'm') {
62         p++;
63       }
64       if (*p == 'm') {
65         p++; // Skip the 'm'
66       }
67       continue;
68     }
69 
70     utf8proc_int32_t codepoint;
71     utf8proc_ssize_t len = utf8proc_iterate(p, -1, &codepoint);
72 
73     if (len <= 0) {
74       // End of string or invalid UTF-8 sequence, stop processing
75       break;
76     }
77 
78     // Get display width of this codepoint
79     int char_width = utf8proc_charwidth(codepoint);
80     if (char_width < 0) {
81       // Control character or unprintable - treat as 0 width
82       char_width = 0;
83     }
84     width += char_width;
85     p += len;
86   }
87 
88   return width;
89 }






Referenced by display_height(), display_width(),
    layout_print_two_column_row(),
    options_config_calculate_max_col_width(), prompt_password(),
    truncate_utf8_with_ellipsis(), and
  validate_palette_chars().






int
  utf8_display_width_n (const char * str, size_t max_bytes)


Definition at line 91 of file utf8.c.


91                                                             {
92   if (!str || max_bytes == 0) {
93     return 0;
94   }
95 
96   int width = 0;
97   const utf8proc_uint8_t *p = (const utf8proc_uint8_t *)str;
98   const utf8proc_uint8_t *end = p + max_bytes;
99 
100   while (p < end && *p) {
101     // Skip ANSI escape sequences (ESC [ ... m)
102     if (p + 1 < end && p[0] == 0x1b && p[1] == '[') {
103       // Found ANSI escape sequence start
104       p += 2; // Skip ESC[
105       // Skip digits and semicolons until we find 'm'
106       while (p < end && *p && *p != 'm') {
107         p++;
108       }
109       if (p < end && *p == 'm') {
110         p++; // Skip the 'm'
111       }
112       continue;
113     }
114 
115     utf8proc_int32_t codepoint;
116     utf8proc_ssize_t len = utf8proc_iterate(p, end - p, &codepoint);
117 
118     if (len <= 0) {
119       // End of string or invalid UTF-8 sequence
120       break;
121     }
122 
123     // Get display width of this codepoint
124     int char_width = utf8proc_charwidth(codepoint);
125     if (char_width < 0) {
126       // Control character or unprintable - treat as 0 width
127       char_width = 0;
128     }
129     width += char_width;
130     p += len;
131   }
132 
133   return width;
134 }






Referenced by utf8_palette_create().






bool utf8_is_ascii_only (const char * str)


Definition at line 165 of file utf8.c.


165                                          {
166   if (!str) {
167     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "str is NULL");
168     return false;
169   }
170 
171   // Fast path: Check if all bytes are in ASCII range (0x00-0x7F)
172   // ASCII characters are 0x00-0x7F (single byte, high bit clear)
173   // Multi-byte UTF-8 has continuation bytes with high bit set (10xxxxxx pattern)
174   // So if all bytes have high bit clear, it's guaranteed ASCII-only
175   const unsigned char *p = (const unsigned char *)str;
176   while (*p) {
177     if ((*p & ~0x7F) != 0) {
178       return false; // Non-ASCII byte found (high bit set)
179     }
180     p++;
181   }
182   return true;
183 }






References SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by is_session_string(), and
    options_init().






bool utf8_is_valid (const char * str)


Definition at line 156 of file utf8.c.


156                                     {
157   if (!str) {
158     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "str is NULL");
159     return false;
160   }
161   // Reuse utf8_char_count to validate without duplicating loop
162   return utf8_char_count(str) != (size_t)-1;
163 }

int
  utf8_next_char_bytes (const char * str, size_t max_bytes)


Definition at line 205 of file utf8.c.


205                                                             {
206   if (!str || max_bytes == 0) {
207     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "str is NULL or max_bytes is 0");
208     return -1;
209   }
210 
211   // Use utf8proc_iterate to get byte length of next character
212   utf8proc_int32_t codepoint;
213   utf8proc_ssize_t len = utf8proc_iterate((const utf8proc_uint8_t *)str, (utf8proc_ssize_t)max_bytes, &codepoint);
214 
215   if (len <= 0) {
216     return -1; // Invalid UTF-8 or end of string
217   }
218 
219   return (int)len;
220 }

int
  utf8_read_and_insert_continuation_bytes (char * buffer, size_t * cursor,
  size_t * len, size_t max_len, int continuation_bytes, int(* read_byte_fn
  )(void))


Definition at line 238 of file utf8.c.


239                                                                                                {
240   if (!buffer || !cursor || !len || continuation_bytes <= 0 || !read_byte_fn) {
241     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "invalid params");
242     return -1;
243   }
244 
245   for (int i = 0; i < continuation_bytes && *len < max_len - 1; i++) {
246     int next_byte = read_byte_fn();
247     if (next_byte == EOF) {
248       return -1; // EOF reached
249     }
250 
251     // Shift characters right to make room
252     memmove(&buffer[*cursor + 1], &buffer[*cursor], *len - *cursor);
253     buffer[*cursor] = (char)next_byte;
254     (*len)++;
255     (*cursor)++;
256   }
257 
258   return 0;
259 }






References SET_ERRNO.






const
  char * utf8_strcasestr (const char * haystack, const char * needle)


Case-insensitive substring search with full Unicode support. Uses
    utf8proc for Unicode case folding according to Unicode standard. This
    properly handles all Unicode scripts including Greek, Cyrillic, accented
    characters, and more.


Definition at line 272 of file utf8.c.


272                                                                       {
273   if (!haystack || !needle) {
274     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "invalid params");
275     return NULL;
276   }
277 
278   // Empty needle matches at start of haystack
279   if (needle[0] == '\0') {
280     return haystack;
281   }
282 
283   // Get lengths
284   size_t haystack_len = strlen(haystack);
285   size_t needle_len = strlen(needle);
286 
287   if (needle_len > haystack_len) {
288     return NULL;
289   }
290 
291   // Case-fold both strings using utf8proc
292   // UTF8PROC_CASEFOLD performs Unicode case folding
293   // UTF8PROC_STABLE ensures stable output
294   // UTF8PROC_COMPOSE normalizes composed characters
295   utf8proc_option_t options = UTF8PROC_CASEFOLD | UTF8PROC_STABLE | UTF8PROC_COMPOSE;
296 
297   // Case-fold the needle (pattern to search for)
298   utf8proc_uint8_t *needle_folded = NULL;
299   utf8proc_ssize_t needle_folded_len =
300       utf8proc_map((const utf8proc_uint8_t *)needle, (utf8proc_ssize_t)needle_len, &needle_folded, options);
301 
302   if (needle_folded_len < 0 || !needle_folded) {
303     // Invalid UTF-8 in needle
304     if (needle_folded) {
305       free(needle_folded);
306     }
307     return NULL;
308   }
309 
310   // Try each position in haystack
311   const char *haystack_pos = haystack;
312   while (*haystack_pos != '\0') {
313     // Calculate remaining haystack length
314     size_t remaining = haystack_len - (size_t)(haystack_pos - haystack);
315 
316     if (remaining < needle_len) {
317       // Not enough characters left to match
318       break;
319     }
320 
321     // Case-fold the current haystack window
322     utf8proc_uint8_t *haystack_folded = NULL;
323     utf8proc_ssize_t haystack_folded_len =
324         utf8proc_map((const utf8proc_uint8_t *)haystack_pos, (utf8proc_ssize_t)needle_len, &haystack_folded, options);
325 
326     if (haystack_folded_len >= 0 && haystack_folded) {
327       // Compare case-folded strings
328       if ((size_t)haystack_folded_len == (size_t)needle_folded_len &&
329           memcmp(haystack_folded, needle_folded, (size_t)needle_folded_len) == 0) {
330         // Match found!
331         free(haystack_folded);
332         free(needle_folded);
333         return haystack_pos;
334       }
335       free(haystack_folded);
336     }
337 
338     // Move to next UTF-8 character in haystack
339     utf8proc_int32_t codepoint;
340     utf8proc_ssize_t bytes = utf8proc_iterate((const utf8proc_uint8_t *)haystack_pos, -1, &codepoint);
341     if (bytes <= 0) {
342       // Invalid UTF-8, move by one byte
343       haystack_pos++;
344     } else {
345       haystack_pos += bytes;
346     }
347   }
348 
349   free(needle_folded);
350   return NULL;
351 }






References SET_ERRNO.

    grep_should_output(), log_search_gather_and_filter_logs(), and
    log_search_get_match_info().






size_t
  utf8_to_codepoints (const char * str, uint32_t * out_codepoints, size_t
  max_codepoints)


Definition at line 185 of file utf8.c.


185                                                                                             {
186   if (!str || !out_codepoints || max_codepoints == 0) {
187     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "str is NULL or out_codepoints is NULL or max_codepoints is 0");
188     return 0;
189   }
190 
191   size_t count = 0;
192   const uint8_t *p = (const uint8_t *)str;
193   while (*p && count < max_codepoints) {
194     uint32_t codepoint;
195     int decode_len = utf8_decode(p, &codepoint);
196     if (decode_len < 0) {
197       return -1; // SIZE_MAX - Invalid UTF-8
198     }
199     out_codepoints[count++] = codepoint;
200     p += decode_len;
201   }
202   return count;
203 }






References SET_ERRNO, and utf8_decode().


Referenced by levenshtein(), and
    validate_palette_chars().






void
  utf8_write_lowercase (FILE * output, const char * text)


Write UTF-8 string to file with ASCII lowercase conversion.
    Converts ASCII uppercase to lowercase while preserving all UTF-8 multi-byte
    sequences unchanged. This ensures proper handling of emoji and non-ASCII
    characters while still performing ASCII case conversion.


Definition at line 360 of file utf8.c.


360                                                           {
361   if (!text) {
362     return;
363   }
364 
365   for (const unsigned char *p = (const unsigned char *)text; *p; p++) {
366     // Check if this is the start of a multi-byte UTF-8 sequence
367     if (*p >= 0xC0) {
368       // Multi-byte UTF-8 character - copy as-is without modification
369       fputc(*p, output);
370       // Copy continuation bytes (0x80-0xBF)
371       while (p[1] >= 0x80 && p[1] < 0xC0) {
372         fputc(*++p, output);
373       }
374     } else if (*p >= 'A' && *p <= 'Z') {
375       // ASCII uppercase - convert to lowercase
376       fputc(*p + 32, output);
377     } else {
378       // ASCII lowercase or control character - copy as-is
379       fputc(*p, output);
380     }
381   }
382 }
